mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL above 250 indicates high inflation, prompting monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both GDPC1 and INDPRO show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: In high unemployment regimes (UNRATE > 5%), monetary policy easing (FEDFUNDS decline) fails to stimulate equity markets, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 1% in 60% of instances where UNRATE > 5% and FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) indicate a healthy labor market, boosting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of months with PAYEMS growth > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 35% of periods where INDPRO shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Increased housing starts (HOUST) signal strong demand for housing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods with HOUST growth > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS) creates uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 1% in 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 245 and FEDFUNDS < 1.5%.
